title: ‘We do feel like we’re being invaded’: Nantucket agrees to try restricted hours at popular tourist destination
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Nantucket's Select Board approved reduced hours for the Sconset Bluff Walk due to resident complaints about tourist behavior.
New visiting hours for the bluff walk are 8:30 a.m. to 7:30 p.m., adjusted from the previous 7:30 a.m. to sunset.
A "bluff docent" will educate visitors during July and August, funded by local associations and homeowners.

title: Queue these up: What to watch on Netflix and more in March
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix adds 'The Lego Movie', 'Misery', and 'Zombieland' to its March 2026 streaming lineup.
'The Secret Agent', 'It Was Just an Accident', and 'Elio' are Oscar-nominated films now available on Hulu and Disney+.
Boston.com staff recommend 'A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms', 'Nonnas', 'Merlin', and 'Wonder Man' for streaming.

title: Why he’s running the Boston Marathon in memory of family treated at Dana-Farber
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Trevor Murray, 30, from Malden, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.
Murray is honoring his father and sister-in-law, both treated at Dana-Farber and who passed away in 2020.
His father ran the 2018 Marathon during cancer treatment, inspiring Trevor's participation this year.

title: MBTA announces schedule changes, detours as nearly 1 million expected to attend Sunday’s St. Patrick’s Day parade
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: MBTA will adjust Red Line to rush hour schedule from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. on Sunday.
Shuttle buses will operate between South Station and South Boston from 9:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m.
Bus routes 9 and 11 suspended on parade day; Routes 10, 16, 17, and 47 will be detoured.

title: Boston Public Schools graduation rate reached all-time high last year, still lags behind state average
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston Public Schools' graduation rate reached 81.3% in 2025, the highest in its history, despite lagging behind the state average.
Graduation rates improved across all demographics, with nearly 5% gains for English learners and over 5% for students with disabilities.
The dropout rate fell to a record low of 3.6%, attributed to enhanced instruction, student supports, and expanded college pathways.

title: Bruins’ playoff hopes are destined to fail if they can’t solve confounding road woes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins currently have an 11-14-5 record in away games, impacting their playoff prospects.
12 of the Bruins' last 21 regular-season games are on the road, complicating their playoff push.
Despite a strong home record, the Bruins face a seven-game road losing streak, crucial against playoff rivals.

title: This Marblehead man is running the Boston Marathon for his pediatric patients
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brian Wishart, 42, from Marblehead, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for his pediatric patients at Spaulding Rehab.
Wishart aims to support the hospital's adaptive sports programs and integrate Spaulding's cutting-edge research into clinical care.
As Chief of Pediatric Rehabilitation Medicine, Wishart works to improve resources and educational support for pediatric patients.

title: 8 children have now died from flu in Mass. this season
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Eight children have died from the flu in Massachusetts this season, with the latest death reported between Feb. 15 and Feb. 21.
As of Feb. 28, 298 adults and eight children in Massachusetts have died from influenza, according to state health data.
Health officials urge flu vaccination, with 38.1% of Massachusetts residents vaccinated as of February end, despite low flu activity.

title: NBA cancels Hawks’ ‘Magic City’ promotion amid backlash following Luke Kornet’s letter
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The NBA canceled the Hawks' 'Magic City Monday' promotion after backlash and concerns from league stakeholders.
Luke Kornet's open letter criticized the Hawks for planning a promotion with Atlanta's Magic City strip club.
Kornet, a former Celtics center, now plays for the Spurs, whom the Celtics play next in San Antonio.

title: Shaquille O’Neal had no clue who Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman is
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Shaquille O'Neal was unaware of Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman until seeing highlights of a game against the Cavaliers.
Baylor Scheierman, a first-round pick in 2024, averages 4.8 points and 3.2 rebounds per game for the Celtics this season.
Despite a thumb fracture, Scheierman continues to play significant minutes, helping fill in during Jayson Tatum's limited play.
